page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The CPU includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Unlike standard general-purpose instances, the m6idn.xlarge includes 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average single-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng single-core score of 1892.08 and Geekbench single-core score of 1601.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3862.58 and a large-block memory read bandwidth of 30.03 GB/sec.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the m6idn.xlarge is defined by its inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage and dedicated CPU allocation within a general-purpose profile. While multi-threaded processing and memory-intensive operations show lower relative performance, the instance maintains average throughput for single-threaded compression, text processing, and static web serving. This profile is suitable for workloads requiring a balance of compute and local storage, such as web serving, moderate database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average single-core CPU performance and average static web serving throughput of 6.34 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring fast local temporary storage. This instance is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and moderate database workloads that benefit from dedicated compute resources and local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, 237 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and a 6 Gbps baseline network, suitable for web serving and database workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, integrated local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced compute resources.

16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with AWS Nitro hypervisor optimization

Suitable for web serving, moderate database operations, and general-purpose workloads
